scope.summary: Non-JS async runtime obligation attribution now consumes Python asyncio imported bindings and Rust brace-use evidence-only imported bindings before attaching shared task and async aggregate obligation labels.

capability_alignment.capability: compose existing ImportedBinding proof with shared async runtime obligations
not_a_feature_list: No new SourceProtocolKind, SourceCallKind, semantic-pack row, or public kernel API was added.
No exact admission path was opened for Python or Rust async runtime APIs.
Python from-import support requires import-backed asyncio binding proof plus local module and same-file shadow guards.
Rust brace-use support requires builtin ImportedBinding evidence in the same module scope, local runtime-root guards, and macro-invocation evidence for join/select aggregates.
Nested Python function-local imports stay closed until scope proof is available.
reused_capabilities: Rust ImportedBinding symbol evidence, including evidence-only brace-use imports
AdmissionContext local Python module and Rust runtime-root guards
shared task, timer, and async aggregate missing-evidence labels

new_reporting_surfaces.guard: The callee must resolve to an import-backed asyncio binding, with no path-visible local asyncio module and no same-file parameter or assignment shadow for that binding.
blocked_false_open_examples: from asyncio import gather as all_done; async def main(all_done, task): return await all_done(task)
from asyncio import sleep as delay; async def main(): delay = local_sleep; return await delay(1)
async def helper(): from asyncio import sleep as delay; async def main(): return await delay(1)
asyncio.py plus from asyncio import create_task as spawn_task in a path-visible sibling or child module

new_reporting_surfaces.surface: use runtime::{spawn/join/select [as local]}; local(...) or local!(...)
new_reporting_surfaces.guard: The callee must have builtin ImportedBinding evidence for the runtime module/export in the same module scope, the binding must not be block-scoped, the runtime root must not be locally defined in the same file, and join/select aggregates must be macro invocations.
blocked_false_open_examples: use tokio::{spawn}; fn run<F>(spawn: F) { spawn(work()) }
use tokio::{spawn}; let spawn = local; spawn(work())
use tokio::{join}; macro_rules! join { ... } join!(a, b)
fn helper() { use tokio::{spawn}; } fn run() { spawn(work()) }
fn run() { spawn(work()) } mod helper { use tokio::{spawn}; fn helper() { spawn(work()) } }
const INIT: () = { use tokio::{spawn}; }; fn run() { spawn(work()) }

assertions: from asyncio import create_task as spawn_task; spawn_task(...) reports task spawn, handle lifecycle, and cancellation/liveness obligations
from asyncio import sleep as delay; delay(...) reports timer scheduling obligations
from asyncio import gather as all_done; all_done(...) reports all-completion aggregate obligations
from asyncio import wait; wait(...) reports completion, cancellation/liveness, and result-channel obligations
use tokio::{spawn}; spawn(...) reports task spawn, handle lifecycle, and cancellation/liveness obligations
use tokio::{join as tokio_join}; tokio_join!(...) reports all-completion aggregate obligations
use futures::{select as fut_select}; fut_select!(...) reports first-completion, cancellation/liveness, and result-channel obligations
Python imported asyncio bindings shadowed by parameters or assignments do not report runtime obligations
Python asyncio bindings imported only in another local scope do not report runtime obligations
Rust brace-imported runtime names shadowed by parameters, let bindings, or local macros do not report runtime obligations
Rust direct or brace runtime imports in another function, block, or module do not report runtime obligations outside that scope
path-visible local asyncio.py blocks from-import backed asyncio task reporting
same-file local mod tokio blocks brace-imported spawn reporting

corpus_pricing_delta.reason: The pricing script now counts Python asyncio from-import bindings in addition to the prior alias/import rows. The pinned 120-repo corpus contributes 2 new Python imported sleep occurrences. Rust brace-use imports were already priced by the imported Rust rows; this slice makes their evidence-only bindings actionable in admission.

validation_commands: cargo run -q -p nose-cli -- verify /tmp/nose-rust-block-import.XXXXXX --recall-loss-report target/recall-loss.rust-block-import-repro.json # verified task-spawn-scheduling-contract count = 0 for const-block use repro

next_exact_work_policy.summary: The reporter can now attribute more imported runtime API spellings, but exact recovery still requires dependency-closed scheduling, cancellation, lifecycle, aggregate result-channel, exception, and callback/effect contracts.
